feed_content: 01 Welcome to pglite-feed — a browser-only microblog powered by PGlite (Postgres in WebAssembly)
02 All data lives in your browser's IndexedDB. Nothing is sent to a server.
03 Add [?admin](?admin) to the URL for edit mode — create, edit, delete entries
04 Type # in the search bar to see a tag cloud of all hashtag categories. The x clears the search and returns to this view.
05 Use [markdown links](https://example.com) or bare URLs https://example.com — both become clickable
06 Data saves automatically to your browser. Use the down-arrow to export a JSON backup, up-arrow to load one (for transferring to other browsers or restoring).
07 Search supports multi-word AND matching. Use -term to exclude. Use hashtags to categorize. Use after: and before: for date filtering.
08 Create a content.json file to override this default content. The app checks for content.json first, falls back to feed.json.
09 Read more [about this app](public/about.md) — rendered inline with the retro theme
10 Fork this repo and deploy to your own domain — [pglite-feed on GitHub](https://github.com/robertvigil/pglite-feed)
This is a sample tagged entry — search for it with: #howto #demo
Tagged entries are hidden from the default view. Search or type # to find them. #howto #demo
